text.title: AT Testing Rules, Antipatterns, and Agentic Workflow
text.summary: Agentic workflow for AT testing, known antipatterns from LLM-assisted accessibility work, and CI gate script reference.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Agents can: run automated scans in CI (axe-core, pa11y, Lighthouse), cross-check ARIA against accessibility-tree snapshots, generate keyboard-traversal scripts, triage findings into severity buckets. Agents cannot reliably evaluate screen-reader audio — capture it via human reviewer or video recording. Reserve human time for: SR audio QA, switch-control testing, lived-experience sessions.

Recommended subagents: faion-usability-agent (runs automated suites, cross-references findings, drafts remediation tickets), faion-ux-researcher-agent (recruits and runs sessions with AT users), faion-sdd-executor-agent (fixes flagged issues with ARIA/semantic-HTML patches, re-runs gates).
section.reference: CI gate: fail PR if axe finds serious/critical WCAG 2.2 AA violations

antipattern.description: Agent declares "WCAG AA conformant" based on axe-core results alone.
antipattern.reason: Automated tools catch 30–50% of real issues. Claiming conformance requires documented testing against all applicable success criteria, including manual SR checks. Always reject this conclusion in prompt rules.

antipattern.description: LLM inventing ARIA patterns without citation to ARIA Authoring Practices Guide.
antipattern.reason: Invented ARIA patterns produce non-standard keyboard behavior and incorrect SR announcements. Every ARIA pattern must cite a specific APG example URL; refuse output without citation.

antipattern.description: Color-contrast checks performed via static analysis on overlapping translucent layers.
antipattern.reason: LLM static analysis cannot compute effective contrast of translucent overlapping elements. Must run in browser against the rendered DOM.

antipattern.description: Testing only initial render state for dynamic content (aria-live, SPA route changes).
antipattern.reason: Dynamic content is the primary false-pass area. Focus must be tested post-state: after dialog opens, after route change, after async content loads.

rule.statement: Capture screen reader audio (OBS, screen recorder) when filing AT bugs. Never rely on text descriptions alone.
rule.rationale: SR announcement strings vary across NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver versions. Agents quote outdated phrasings. Video recordings are the only evidence that resolves SR behavior disputes with developers.

rule.statement: Build a regression catalog: components with known AT bugs get re-tested on every release, not only when the component changes.
rule.rationale: Adjacent changes in CSS or JavaScript frequently reintroduce fixed AT bugs. A regression catalog with test scripts catches this without manual re-discovery each cycle.
